title: Windows GUI, Administrative Tools, Settings, and State Inspection · Guided Service Studio
summary: A guided support bench extends Windows state inspection and least-change plan while retaining authorization, safety, baseline, privacy, accessibility, rollback, verification, documentation, and user communication. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of Windows state inspection and least-change plan while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Locate and explain current Settings, Control Panel, search, taskbar, notification, accessibility, power, display, application, account, and update surfaces.
Select Task Manager, Computer Management, Event Viewer, Device Manager, Disk Management, Services, System Configuration, Resource Monitor, Registry Editor, and policy tools by evidence need.
Distinguish application, process, service, scheduled task, startup entry, dependency, account, trigger, resource, and termination state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Windows Navigation
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Startup delay, storage pressure, device errors, and update failures send support through many tools without recording effective state or time. The workshop variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Windows Navigation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for locate and explain current settings, control panel, search, taskbar, notification, accessibility, power, display, application, account, and update surfaces. For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Windows Navigation, keep Windows Navigation connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Startup delay, storage pressure, device errors, and update failures send support through many tools without recording effective state or time. The workshop variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Windows Navigation condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: CompTIA A+ Core 2 220-1202 Exam Objectives — Controls every current Core 2 operating-system, security, software-troubleshooting, and operational-procedure objective and weighting.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Windows Navigation, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Windows Navigation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Windows Navigation, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Startup delay, storage pressure, device errors, and update failures send support through many tools without recording effective state or time. The workshop variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Windows Navigation, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: A Windows setting exists in one permanent location across every supported release.

options.text: A Windows setting exists in one permanent location across every supported release. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Windows Navigation.

For commit to the mission model, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof. This does not satisfy Windows Navigation for Windows state inspection and least-change plan.
State the Windows Navigation preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.

content.code:
support-event-change-evidence-nvmtx9:
  user-impact: [replace with the bounded user outcome]
  safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, and stop rule]
  hypothesis: [replace with a falsifiable cause and competing explanation]
  test: [replace with one reversible discriminating simulator test]
